title: Fatal error on split variable when tracing is enabled
body:
Hi there.

The following example code:
```
module test
  (
   input      [10:0] [31:0]     register_in,
    output     [10:0] [31:0]     register
   );
   assign register = register_in;

endmodule

module verilator_split;

   logic [10:0] [31:0] register_in /*verilator split_var*/;

   test test
     (.register_in(register_in),
      .register());
endmodule
```

Leads to the following compilation error when tracing is enabled:
```
verilator --cc -O3 --trace-fst verilator_split.sv --top-module verilator_split --exe verilator_split.cpp
%Error: Internal Error: verilator_split.sv:15:20: ../V3SplitVar.cpp:1124: not enough split variables
                                                : ... note: In instance 'verilator_split'
   15 |      (.register_in(register_in),
      |                    ^~~~~~~~~~~
                        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance.
```

Without the `--trace-fst` flag it works properly.

Quickly going through the code it seems to be related to the fact that some undriven signals  don't get optimised out when tracing is enabled and I'm therefore wondering if this assert in V3SplitVar.cpp:1124 is correct in this case?

Thanks

Adrien
